title: Basler Leckerli
subtitle: Swiss spiced honey and almond bars from Basel
excerpt: Basler Leckerli are dense, chewy spiced bars from the Swiss city of Basel, made from a boiled honey-and-sugar mixture with almonds, mixed peel, and warming spice, baked in a single slab and cut into rectangles while warm. Glazed with a thin lemon icing. They are a Christmas market staple but keep for weeks in a tin.
sourceNotes: Basler Leckerli have been produced in Basel for several centuries and are protected as a traditional regional speciality. The recipe appears in early Swiss confectionery records and has been commercially produced by Basel bakeries since the eighteenth century. The distinguishing features are the boiled honey base, almonds, mixed peel, and Kirsch, and the final lemon glaze. This recipe follows the traditional domestic format without Kirsch, which is optionally included.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Basler Leckerli improve significantly after 2 to 3 days in a tin: the spice deepens and the texture becomes chewier. They keep for up to 4 weeks in a sealed tin.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the slab bakes in 18 to 22 minutes; look for a set, dry surface with lightly golden edges

glossaryTerms.definition: Leckerli dough starts with honey and sugar heated together until the sugar fully dissolves and the mixture boils. This is not caramelising: the temperature is relatively low (around 90 to 95 degrees C) and the honey should not colour. The hot liquid is poured over the dry ingredients, which partly hydrates the flour and helps the mixture come together into a firm, workable mass. The mixture will feel stiff and dry at first; continue working it until it is evenly combined.

content.text: Basler Leckerli are made by the slab, cut hot, and improved by resting. The

makes the dough unusually stiff. Press it into the tin using the back of a wet spoon rather than rolling. Cut the slab into bars as soon as it comes out of the oven, then glaze immediately so the icing sticks.

Heat the oven to 200 degrees C fan. Line a 30x20 cm baking tin with baking paper. Heat 250 g honey and 200 g caster sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture just reaches a boil. Remove from heat. In a large bowl, mix together 350 g flour, 200 g chopped almonds, 100 g mixed peel, lemon and orange zest, 2 tsp mixed spice, 1 tsp cinnamon, 1 tsp ginger, and 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda. Pour the hot honey mixture over the dry ingredients and stir vigorously until a stiff, sticky dough forms. Press evenly into the prepared tin using the back of a wet spoon. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until the surface is dry and the edges are lightly golden.

As soon as the slab comes out of the oven, cut it immediately into bars roughly 3x6 cm while still in the tin. Leave in the tin for 10 minutes. Make the glaze by whisking 150 g icing sugar with 2 tbsp lemon juice to a thick, pourable consistency. Brush generously over the warm bars while they are still in the tin. Leave to set for 30 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool completely.

items.problem: Slab is too hard to cut cleanly
items.solution: The leckerli cooled too long before cutting. Cut immediately from the oven using a sharp knife. If they have hardened, warm briefly in the oven for 3 minutes and cut again.

items.problem: Bars are very hard and dry after a day or two
items.solution: They need to be stored in an airtight tin rather than exposed to air. Add half a slice of apple or a bread crust to the tin to provide a little moisture. The apple or crust can be removed after 24 hours once the texture has softened.
